data.title: Oracle Stock Just Jumped Again. Here's What One Investor Sees Next
data.description:
Oracle (NASDAQ:ORCL) closed the week with a bang, with shares rising 9% on Friday after climbing nearly 7% in the previous session. The rally appeared to follow Snowflake's stronger-than-expected earnings report and upbeat outlook, which helped ease concerns about enterprise technology spending. The results suggested companies remain willing to invest in cloud platforms, data infrastructure, and AI-related initiatives, lifting sentiment across the software sector and helping power Oracle's advance.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

One investor remaining constructive on Oracle's long-term prospects is Justin Purohit, who believes the cloud computing giant is steadily establishing itself as a critical provider of AI infrastructure rather than simply a legacy software company.

According to Purohit, much of the company's opportunity lies within its rapidly expanding infrastructure business. The investor believes Oracle is positioning itself as "a critical infrastructure provider rather than simply a legacy software company," pointing to continued demand for AI computing capacity and the company's growing role in supporting enterprise AI workloads.

Purohit also sees Oracle's relationships with Microsoft, Google, and Amazon as an important part of the story. The investor expects those partnerships to help the company capture additional cloud and database demand over time while strengthening its position within the broader AI ecosystem. He also noted that management's decision to raise long-term guidance and highlight demand trends running ahead of previous expectations lends additional support to the bullish thesis.

Looking ahead to Oracle's upcoming earnings report in June, Purohit believes investors should focus less on the headline numbers and more on whether management can validate the exceptionally strong outlook presented last quarter. "Last quarter was a great quarter, and the forward commentary was arguably even stronger than the numbers themselves," he said. "I believe the challenge now becomes living up to those expectations."

One area Purohit plans to monitor closely is whether demand continues outpacing available capacity. "The AI infrastructure business will likely remain front and center," he wrote, adding that investors will likely be looking for updates on capacity expansion, backlog conversion, and overall demand trends. From his perspective, Oracle's backlog represents one of the company's greatest strengths, but he would like more clarity regarding how quickly that demand can be converted into reported revenue.

Beyond infrastructure, Purohit also believes Oracle's Fusion and NetSuite businesses deserve attention. He argues that continued double-digit growth in those segments would reinforce the idea that Oracle is evolving into a more balanced cloud company rather than relying solely on AI-related infrastructure demand.

Despite his constructive outlook, Purohit stops short of becoming outright bullish on the shares. He believes Oracle's expansion strategy requires significant capital commitments and that expectations surrounding long-term AI demand remain ambitious. While the investor sees considerable merit in the bullish argument surrounding Oracle's infrastructure opportunity, he cautions that investors should not rush into what appears to be a compelling growth story without considering the risks.

For that reason, Purohit continues to rate Oracle shares a Hold (i.e., Neutral) ahead of earnings. Although he believes the company is well positioned to benefit if AI demand remains robust, he also warns that slower enterprise spending, tighter financing conditions, or moderation in customer demand could lead investors to reassess the stock's outlook. (To watch Purohit's track record, click here)

Wall Street appears considerably more confident. Oracle boasts a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 28 Buy and 5 Hold recommendations. The average 12-month price target of $248.82 points to about 10% upside from current levels. (See ORCL stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Stock (HPE) Rallies Thanks to Dell's AI Boom
data.description:
Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) stock soared on Friday despite a lack of news from the computer company. Instead, HPE investors can thank Dell Technologies (DELL) for the boost. This is due to Dell's latest earnings report released today. A highlight from that report was an updated outlook for Fiscal 2027.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Dell increased its revenue guidance for Fiscal 2027 to $167 billion, compared to its prior guidance of $140 billion. The company's updated revenue guidance is also well above Wall Street's estimate of $142.1 billion. This updated revenue guidance is due to increased demand for servers. The need for additional servers came from data centers that power AI.

Dell, being the latest company to benefit from the AI boom, has investors turning to Hewlett Packard Enterprise. Hewlett Packard Enterprise serves the same server market, leading some investors to believe it will also rise on increased demand. It won't take long to find out if that's true. The company is set to report Q2 2026 earnings after markets close on Monday, June 1, 2026. Wall Street expects adjusted earnings per share of 54 cents on revenue of $9.78 billion.

Hewlett Packard Enterprise Stock Movement Today

Hewlett Packard Enterprise stock was up nearly 9% on Friday, extending a 74.43% year-to-date rally. The stock has also soared 121.12% over the past 12 months.

HPE stock trading activity today saw some 10 million shares change hands. That's closing in on the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 15.74 million shares.

Is Hewlett Packard Enterprise Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Hewlett Packard Enterprise is Moderate Buy, based on eight Buy and four Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average HPE stock price target of $32.36, representing a potential 23.4% upside for the shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Jumps on Positive News from Xbox
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) stock was on the rise Friday following news from Xbox CEO Asha Sharma. The Xbox gaming division head said that Xbox's recent price drop for Game Pass has performed well. According to the CEO, the subscription service has seen "acquisitions grow and retention improve" after the price cuts. Sharma introduced the lower price for Game Pass after growth slowed and subscriber loss accelerated following a price increase last year.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

However, it's not all sunshine and rainbows over at Xbox. The company is in a tough spot right now, as the AI boom has caused shortages of computer memory and storage. This has also affected rival game companies. One example of this is Valve, which had to massively increase the price of its handheld gaming PC, Steam Deck, due to the shortages.

Sharma also warned employees in a memo that "tough decisions are ahead." While that seems alarming, the CEO said that all of this will be in service of creating a stronger Xbox. Sharma said Xbox has to determine the type of company it will become, and this could bring major changes to the business.

Microsoft Stock Movement Today

Microsoft stock was up 3.92% on Friday, but was still down 7.84% year-to-date. The stock has also fallen 7.25% over the past 12 months.

MSFT stock movement today saw some 25.69 million shares change hands. This is below the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 34.71 million shares.

Is Microsoft Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Microsoft is Strong Buy, based on 34 Buy and two Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average MSFT stock price target of $556.88, representing a potential 25.5% upside for the shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Why Is Penny Stock PRF Technologies (PRFX) Up Today?
data.description:
PRF Technologies (PRFX) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the AI company provided an update on the development of DeepSolar Predict. This is the company's AI-driven renewable energy revenue optimization platform. The DeepSolar Predict platform is designed to help renewable energy operators move from production monitoring and standalone forecasting to real-time revenue optimization. It does so by combining AI forecasting, storage intelligence, and decision support.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

PRF Technologies revealed that DeepSolar Predict is advancing toward a commercial release. This would allow the company to generate revenue from the platform and help recoup the costs of its creation. Additionally, the company said that DeepSolar Predict builds on its other initiatives, including its participation in the Nvidia (NVDA) Connect program. Nvidia Connect is a free program that helps software development companies and service providers shorten time to market through tailored development resources.

Dr. Ehud Geller, chairman of PRF Technologies, said the renewable energy market is undergoing a change. That includes accounting for financial exposure and not just energy volume. As such, the company will focus on helping customers "move from reactive operations to proactive, data-driven revenue optimization."

PRF Technologies Stock Movement Today

PRF Technologies stock was up nearly 230% on Friday, following a 1.48% jump yesterday. However, the stock has fallen 57.29% year-to-date and also dropped 85.8% over the past 12 months.

PRFX stock trading activity today saw some 23 million shares change hands. That's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 186,000 shares.

Is PRF Technologies Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of PRFX stock is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Spark rates PRFX stock as Underperform with a $1.50 price target. It cites "weak financial fundamentals (minimal revenue, ongoing losses, and cash burn) and bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD)" as reasons for this stance.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Jump to New Highs as Trump Nears Iran Deal Verdict; Oil Extends Decline
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) rallied to new highs on Friday, rounding out a strong month for stocks.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Trump held a meeting in the Situation Room with top officials to provide a "final determination" on a deal with Iran. He mentioned the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the removal of mines in the waterway, and the destruction of Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium as key conditions for the U.S. to accept the deal.
  • The U.S. would lift its blockade on Iranian ports if the conditions are satisfied and begin negotiating nuclear restrictions for Iran. "No money will be exchanged, until further notice," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "Other items, of far less importance, have been agreed to."
  • Brent Crude oil (BZ) fell by over 1% and secured its largest monthly loss since 2020. Furthermore, U.S. gasoline prices declined 3.5 cents overnight, marking the largest one-day fall in 17 years.
  • Dell (DELL) skyrocketed higher after reporting that its AI server revenue surged 757% to $16.1 billion during the first quarter. The company's overall revenue accelerated by nearly 88%.
  • Software stocks continued to rise, highlighted by strong returns from ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), Datadog (DDOG), Palantir (PLTR), and CrowdStrike (CRWD).
  • Truist analyst Youssef Squali raised his Amazon (AMZN) price target to $320 from $310 and his Alphabet (GOOGL) target to $430 from $415.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: This Is Why Hub Cyber Security Stock (HUBC) Was Up Today
data.description:
Hub Cyber Security (HUBC) stock underwent a massive rally on Friday alongside a new filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This filing revealed a major stake held by an investor in the Israel-based cybersecurity company. According to this filing, Andre Wang holds a 15.6% stake in Hub Cyber Security via his 200,000 shares.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Wang isn't the only investor who has revealed a large stake in Hub Cyber Security stock recently. Tyler White and Jon Walden also revealed their stakes in the company on Wednesday. White holds 450,000 shares, which represent a 35.1% stake in the company. Walden owns 128,022 shares, which grants him a 9.9% stake in Hub Cyber Security.

While these recent shareholder filings may tempt some investors to buy HUBC stock, there are some red flags to note. That includes a noncompliance letter from the Nasdaq (NDAQ). Hub Cyber Security is in violation of Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250. This is because the company has yet to file its Form 20-F annual report for the period ended December 31, 2025 with the SEC.

HUBC Stock Movement Today

Hub Cyber Security stock was up 300% on Friday. However, the shares were still down 99.85% year-to-date and have fallen 99.99% over the past 12 months.

HUBC stock trading activity today was heightened. This saw some 1.16 billion shares change hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 657,000 shares.

Is Hub Cyber Security Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, traditional analyst coverage of Hub Cyber Security is lacking. Fortunately, TipRanks' AI analyst Spark has it covered. Spark rates HUBC stock as Neutral with a $2.50 price target. It cites "severe financial weakness (declining revenue, ongoing losses, negative equity, and negative free cash flow)" as reasons for this stance.
data.author: William White

data.title: S&P 500 Top Stock Gainers Today, 5/29/26
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) advanced to a new record high on Friday, closing out May with a 4.8% gain.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Today's Top-Performing Sector 

Information technology was the top-performing sector on Friday. Dell Technologies (DELL) led the charge after reporting a blowout first-quarter earnings report, highlighted by AI server revenue growth of 757% to $16.1 billion. In addition, software stocks continued to rally higher, with NetApp (NTAP), IBM (IBM), and ServiceNow (NOW) posting strong returns.

Several information technology stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Dell Technologies (DELL)
  • NetApp (NTAP)
  • IBM (IBM)
  • ServiceNow (NOW)
  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the consumer staples sector faced the largest drawdown as investors rotated out of the defensive sector and into more speculative areas of the market. Clorox (CLX) led the group to the downside after CEO Linda Rendle announced she would step down due to health reasons and start a search for the next CEO. Costco (COST) also closed lower after reporting its fiscal third-quarter earnings.

Notable consumer staples stocks trading lower include:

  • Clorox (CLX)
  • Costco (COST)
  • Altria Group (MO)
  • Kimberly-Clark Corp (KMB)
  • Walmart (WMT)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'It's Exceptional,' Says Investor About Palantir Stock
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) closed the week on a strong note, with shares jumping another 9% on Friday after gaining 8% the previous day as Snowflake's earnings helped ease concerns that AI could undermine traditional software businesses. Instead, the results suggested enterprises remain willing to spend heavily on AI tools, data infrastructure, and the software needed to deploy those technologies at scale. As one of the market's most prominent AI-focused software companies, Palantir appeared to benefit from that improving sentiment.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Yet, one investor, known by the pseudonym Alpha Line, believes Palantir's appeal goes beyond simply riding the wave of enterprise AI spending.

"I stay long on PLTR because the company increasingly looks like a foundational AI infrastructure platform with exceptional operating momentum," the investor said.

Alpha Line argues that Palantir's profitability metrics deserve just as much scrutiny as its growth rates. The investor contends that many software businesses experience margin pressure as they scale, whereas Palantir appears to be moving in the opposite direction. He believes this reflects a business model where customer expansion can occur without comparable increases in costs, creating economics that resemble infrastructure platforms more than conventional software companies.

Alpha Line says he is "starting to believe that the time has come to stop comparing Palantir's fundamentals with conventional SaaS firms," arguing that the company is building a platform that becomes deeply embedded within customer operations. In his view, metrics such as customer retention and expansion suggest users are no longer experimenting with Palantir's products but integrating them into critical workflows.

Perhaps the most debated aspect of the Palantir story is its valuation. Alpha Line acknowledges that traditional metrics paint an expensive picture. Nevertheless, he believes investors may be focusing on the wrong variables. Rather than concentrating solely on valuation multiples, the investor contends that the more important question is how rapidly Palantir can grow into those expectations. Alpha Line describes the company's financial profile as "structurally abnormal" compared to what is typically seen among high-growth software businesses.

At the same time, Alpha Line does not dismiss the risks. The investor warns that any moderation in growth could lead to significant volatility, particularly given the ambitious assumptions many market participants have built into their forecasts. He also points to potential challenges related to international growth and the broader AI landscape.

Still, the investor believes the risk-reward balance favors the bulls. "Palantir is no longer proving whether its AI platform works; it is proving how large the compounding effect can become," the investor summed up. Accordingly, Alpha Line rates PLTR shares a Strong Buy. (To watch Alpha Line's track record, click here)

Wall Street is somewhat more cautious, though the overall view remains favorable. Based on 20 recent analyst ratings, PLTR claims a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with 13 analysts recommending Buy, 5 suggesting Hold, and 2 assigning Sell ratings. The average 12-month price target stands at $185.35, implying potential upside of about 18% from current levels. (See PLTR stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Why Did Oracle Stock (ORCL) Jump 11% Today, 5/29/26?
data.description:
Shares in Oracle (ORCL), the enterprise software giant shifting towards becoming an AI cloud company, climbed by 10.84% on Friday, adding to its roughly 7% gain the previous day. The rally coincides with JPMorgan (JPM) kicking off coverage of the stock with a Buy rating and a $210 price target, which implies about 3% upside from the ORCL closing price of $203.70 on Thursday.
